title: वास्तु शान्ति स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वास्तु शान्ति स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
वास्तुनारायणं देवं सर्ववास्तुसमाश्रितम्।
सर्वरक्षाकरं शान्तं वन्दे वास्तुपतिं विभुम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
ॐ वास्तोष्पते प्रति जानीह्यस्मान्
त्स्वावेशो अनमीवो भवा नः।
यत्त्वेमहे प्रति तन्नो जुषस्व
शं नो भव द्विपदे शं चतुष्पदे॥ १॥

वास्तोष्पते प्रतरणो न एधि
गयस्फानो गोभिरश्वेभिरिन्दो।
अजरासस्ते सख्ये स्याम
पितेव पुत्रान्प्रति नो जुषस्व॥ २॥

वास्तोष्पते शग्मया संसदा ते
सक्षीमहि रण्वया गातुमत्या।
आवः क्षेम उत योगे वरं नो
यूयं पात स्वस्तिभिः सदा नः॥ ३॥

॥ वास्तु शान्ति मन्त्र ॥
ॐ शान्तिरस्तु शान्तिरस्तु शान्तिरस्तु।
ॐ वास्तुदोषनाशनाय श्रीवास्तुपुरुषाय नमः॥

ॐ भूमिदेव्यै नमः। ॐ वास्तुदेवाय नमः।
ॐ दिग्पालेभ्यो नमः। ॐ क्षेत्रपालाय नमः।
ॐ गृहलक्ष्म्यै नमः। ॐ इन्द्राय नमः।
ॐ वरुणाय नमः। ॐ कुबेराय नमः।
ॐ यमाय नमः। ॐ ईशानाय नमः॥

॥ गृह शान्ति प्रार्थना ॥
स्वस्ति नो इन्द्रो वृद्धश्रवाः स्वस्ति नः पूषा विश्ववेदाः।
स्वस्ति नस्तार्क्ष्यो अरिष्टनेमिः स्वस्ति नो बृहस्पतिर्दधातु॥

ॐ द्यौः शान्तिरन्तरिक्षं शान्तिः पृथिवी शान्तिरापः शान्ति-
रोषधयः शान्तिर्वनस्पतयः शान्तिर्विश्वेदेवाः शान्ति-
र्ब्रह्म शान्तिः सर्वं शान्तिः शान्तिरेव शान्तिः
सा मा शान्तिरेधि॥

ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ वास्तु पूजा मन्त्र ॥
गृहारम्भे गृहप्रवेशे गृहवासे सर्वदा शुभम्।
वास्तुपूजनमात्रेण सर्वसिद्धिर्भवेद्ध्रुवम्॥

ॐ वास्तुदेवाय शान्तिं कुर्वन्तु।
सर्वग्रहपीडानाशनं कुर्वन्तु।
गृहे सुखशान्तिसमृद्धिं कुर्वन्तु॥

॥ इति श्री वास्तु शान्ति स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vastu Shanti Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vastu-narayanam Devam Sarva-vastu-samashritam.
Sarva-raksha-karam Shantam Vande Vastu-patim Vibhum.

|| Stotram ||
Om Vastoshpate Prati Janihy-asman
Tsva-avesho Anamivo Bhava Nah.
Yat-tvemahe Prati Tanno Jushasva
Sham No Bhava Dvipade Sham Chatushpade. || 1 ||

Vastoshpate Pratarano Na Edhi
Gaya-sphano Gobhir-ashvebhir-indo.
Ajara-sas-te Sakhye Syama
Piteva Putran-prati No Jushasva. || 2 ||

Vastoshpate Shagmaya Samsada Te
Sakshimahi Ranvaya Gatu-matya.
Avah Kshema Uta Yoge Varam No
Yuyam Pata Svastibhih Sada Nah. || 3 ||

|| Vastu Shanti Mantra ||
Om Shantir-astu Shantir-astu Shantir-astu.
Om Vastu-dosha-nashanaya Shri-vastu-purushaya Namah.

Om Bhumi-devyai Namah. Om Vastu-devaya Namah.
Om Dig-palebhyo Namah. Om Kshetra-palaya Namah.
Om Griha-lakshmyai Namah. Om Indraya Namah.
Om Varunaya Namah. Om Kuberaya Namah.
Om Yamaya Namah. Om Ishanaya Namah.

|| Griha Shanti Prarthana ||
Svasti No Indro Vriddha-shravah Svasti Nah Pusha Vishva-vedah.
Svasti Nas-tarkshyo Arishta-nemih Svasti No Brihaspatir-dadhatu.

Om Dyauh Shantir-antarikasham Shantih Prithivi Shantir-apah Shanti-
roshadhayah Shantir-vanaspatayah Shantir-vishvedevah Shanti-
rbrahma Shantih Sarvam Shantih Shantireva Shantih
Sa Ma Shantir-edhi.

Om Shantih Shantih Shantih.

|| Vastu Puja Mantra ||
Griha-arambhe Griha-praveshe Griha-vase Sarvada Shubham.
Vastu-pujanam-atrena Sarva-siddhir-bhaved-dhruvam.

Om Vastu-devaya Shantim Kurvantu.
Sarva-graha-pidaa-nashanam Kurvantu.
Grihe Sukha-shanti-samriddhim Kurvantu.

|| Iti Shri Vastu Shanti Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वास्तु शान्ति स्तोत्र में गृह, भवन और भूमि के वास्तुदोषों के निवारण हेतु वैदिक मन्त्र और प्रार्थनाएँ संकलित हैं।

ऋग्वेद के वास्तोष्पति सूक्त में गृह के स्वामी (वास्तोष्पति) से प्रार्थना है कि हमें स्वस्थ, निरोग और सुखी रखें। हमारे दो पैर वालों (मनुष्यों) और चार पैर वालों (पशुओं) का कल्याण करें। पिता जैसे पुत्रों की रक्षा करता है, वैसे ही हमें स्वीकार करें।

वास्तु शान्ति मन्त्र में भूमि देवी, वास्तुदेव, दिग्पाल, क्षेत्रपाल, गृहलक्ष्मी, इन्द्र, वरुण, कुबेर, यम और ईशान को नमन है।

शान्ति मन्त्र में आकाश, अन्तरिक्ष, पृथ्वी, जल, ओषधियाँ, वनस्पतियाँ, विश्वदेवता और ब्रह्म सबमें शान्ति की प्रार्थना है।

गृहारम्भ, गृहप्रवेश और गृहवास में वास्तुपूजन से सर्वसिद्धि होती है।
benefits: Classical tradition invokes this for removal of vastu doshas (architectural/directional defects) from homes
Contains authentic Vedic Vastoshpati Sukta from Rigveda
Invokes all directional deities for complete home protection
Traditionally associated with peace, prosperity, and harmony to the household
Shanti Mantra pacifies all cosmic disturbances affecting the dwelling
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Vastu Shanti Stotram (वास्तु शान्ति स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Rigveda (7.54.1-3), Yajurveda, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bring peace, prosperity, and harmony to the household and remove all Vastu doshas (architectural/directional defects) from homes. It is also said to invoke all directional deities for complete home protection. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vastu Shanti Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Vastu Shanti Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Vedic Vastoshpati Sukta and prayers for removing Vastu doshas from home.
relatedSearches: Vastu Shanti Stotram lyrics Sanskrit Hindi
Vastu Shanti puja vidhi at home steps
When to perform Vastu Shanti new house

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural basis of Vastu Shanti Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: References to Vastu principles and pacification rites are found in the Rig Veda (the Vastoshpati Sukta of Mandala 7.54-55), the Atharva Veda (which contains specific shanti-mantras for new dwellings), the Matsya Purana, and the Skanda Purana, which provides the most detailed liturgical sequence including invocations, offerings to the directional deities, and the closing pacification verses. The Vishwakarma Prakasha and Mayamatam, the two principal manuals of Vastu Shastra, draw on this Puranic material to codify the Vastu Shanti homa as it is performed today. The stotram in current use is a curated compilation from these sources, varying slightly between Maharashtrian, Tamil and North Indian household manuals. (Sources: Wikipedia Vastu Shastra; Wikipedia Grihapravesha; Shivology Vastu Shanti Puja.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Vastu Shanti puja and stotram recitation be performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Five life-events traditionally trigger Vastu Shanti: when a newly constructed house is ready for occupation, when a family moves into an older purchased or rented home for the first time, after major renovation that alters walls or door positions, after a prolonged absence of the family from a closed home, and following events believed to disturb the Vastu energy such as a death in the house or a fire. The ceremony is scheduled on an auspicious muhurta computed by the family priest using the panchanga, generally on a Thursday or during Uttarayana, and must be completed before the family begins overnight residence. (Sources: GaneshaSpeaks Vastu Shanti; ThePackersMovers Vastu Shanti; Shivology.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the main components of a complete Vastu Shanti ceremony?
seoFaqs.answer: A traditional Vastu Shanti includes Ganesh-puja for obstacle removal, Punyahavachan for purification of the space, navagraha-puja for planetary harmony, Vastu Mandala drawing on the floor with rice flour and turmeric, invocation of Vastu Purusha and the forty-five mandala-deities by name, recitation of Vastu Shanti Stotram and the Vastoshpati Sukta, offering of 108 ahutis into the homa-kunda using ghee, samidha and havan-samagri, milk-boiling on the new kitchen stove (kshira-utsava), feeding of Brahmins and the poor (anna-daan), and a closing aarti. The full ceremony lasts three to six hours depending on the elaborateness of the homa. (Sources: GaneshaSpeaks; ThePackersMovers; Wikipedia Grihapravesha.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do families report from reciting Vastu Shanti Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Documented benefits in traditional sources include neutralisation of vastu-dosha arising from compromises in design or orientation, removal of negative residues left by previous occupants, harmonisation of the five elements within the home, removal of obstacles to financial prosperity, prevention of recurring health issues attributed to specific directional defects, peace among family members, and protection of children and elderly residents. Modern practitioners of energy-mapping report that homes which undergo formal Vastu Shanti show measurable improvement in subjective wellbeing of occupants compared to those that omit the ritual, though the traditional framework attributes results to deva-graha rather than empirical cause. (Sources: ThePackersMovers; Shivology; GaneshaSpeaks.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Vastu Shanti Stotram be recited without performing the full homa?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. While the full Vastu Shanti homa with a qualified priest is the preferred and most efficacious form, many family manuals permit a simplified household recitation in cases where a homa is not feasible. The household form involves bathing, lighting a ghee lamp at the Brahmasthan or in the puja room, sprinkling holy water at the four corners and centre of the home, reciting the Vastu Shanti Stotram once at sunrise on an auspicious day, and offering fruits and flowers to a small representation of Vastu Purusha. This abbreviated form is recommended monthly on the day matching the family's gotra-nakshatra or annually on the griha-pravesh anniversary. (Sources: Astromantra Vastu Purusha; Shivology; TemplePurohit.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Vastu Shanti differ from the Vedic Vastoshpati Sukta?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vastoshpati Sukta of Rig Veda 7.54-55 is the oldest extant Vastu prayer in Sanskrit, addressed to Vastoshpati as the lord of dwelling, asking him to grant a thriving home, healthy cattle and protected progeny. It is short, hymnic and pre-dates the Puranic Vastu Purusha doctrine. The Vastu Shanti Stotram is a Puranic-era expansion that incorporates the Vastu Purusha mythology, names the forty-five mandala-deities, and is integrated into the larger sodasha-upachara puja sequence. Most modern Vastu Shanti rituals open with the Vastoshpati Sukta as a Vedic invocation and then proceed to the Puranic stotram, combining both layers of tradition. (Sources: Wikipedia Vastu Shastra; Wikipedia Grihapravesha; ArtFactory.)
